risks.title: Migration removes target-owned files by mistake
risks.summary: The 1.0 migration deletes copied package-owned files from target repositories. A bad file list could remove project-owned content.
mitigations: Preserve docs/architext/data/*.json by default
Expose removals in --dry-run
Limit cleanup to known copied install paths

risks.title: Agents keep following copied-template instructions
risks.summary: Existing AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md sections may tell agents to edit copied viewer/schema/tool files or run target-local npm commands.
mitigations: Replace the managed Architext section during migration
Make doctor report outdated instruction sections
Prompt agents to edit only docs/architext/data/*.json

risks.title: Installed CLI schema differs from target data expectations
risks.summary: Target data created by an older CLI may fail validation under a newer package-owned schema.
mitigations: Report CLI and metadata versions in doctor
Keep data migrations explicit and dry-runnable
Do not rewrite architecture facts silently

risks.title: Package contents miss runtime files
risks.summary: The global CLI depends on package-owned viewer assets, schemas, and tools. A bad package file list can break serve, build, or validate.
mitigations: Run npm pack --dry-run before release
Keep package files explicit
Validate serve/build/package workflows before publishing

risks.title: Static export becomes stale after data edits
risks.summary: architext build copies target data into dist/data. Later JSON edits are not reflected until build runs again.
mitigations: Keep docs/architext/dist ignored by default
Prefer architext serve for active review
Rebuild before publishing static output

risks.title: Routing complexity regresses readability or performance
risks.summary: Dense diagrams can expose overlapping routes, hidden failures, slow planning, or visual differences between orthogonal, spline, and straight line styles.
mitigations: Keep geometry, ports, route indexing, rendering, and candidate scoring separated
Protect route behavior with fitness tests
Use local Roboticus and Aegis only as informal litmus tests

risks.title: PDF export loses diagram fidelity
risks.summary: PDF export may crop large diagrams, hide overflow-only content, flatten interactive details, or diverge from the browser-rendered viewer.
mitigations: Define export scope and page layout before implementation
Use visual regression fixtures for oversized diagrams
Report route warnings and clipped content during export
